2605.04361.title: When Context Hurts: The Crossover Effect of Knowledge Transfer on Multi-Agent Design Exploration
2605.04361.paper_summary: The prevailing assumption in agent orchestration is that more context is better. We test this on multi-agent software design across 10 tasks, 7 context-injection conditions, and over 2,700 runs, and find a crossover effect: the same artifact type improves design exploration on some tasks (up to 20$\times$ tradeoff coverage) and actively degrades it on others (up to 46% reduction). On several tasks, an irrelevant document performs as well as or better than every relevant artifact. The direction is predicted by a single measurable variable--baseline exploration without context--with Pearson $r = -0.82$ ($p < 0.001$). Probing the mechanism by manipulating convergence pressure through prompt design reveals two distinct regimes: convergence driven by training data priors (natural) responds to artifact disruption, while convergence driven by explicit instructions (induced) does not. The implication is that context injection should be conditional, not universal: one no-context trial is a cheap diagnostic that predicts whether knowledge artifacts will help or hurt a given task.
2605.04361.paper_summary_zh: 當前在代理協調中的假設是，更多的上下文是更好的。我們在10個任務、7個上下文注入條件和超過2,700次運行中測試了這一點，並發現了一個交叉效應：相同的工件類型在某些任務上改善了設計探索（最多20$\times$的權衡覆蓋），而在其他任務上則會主動降低其效果（最多減少46%）。在幾個任務中，一份無關的文檔表現得與每個相關工件一樣好或更好。這一趨勢由一個可測量的變量預測——沒有上下文的基線探索——其皮爾森相關係數 $r = -0.82$ ($p < 0.001$)。通過操控提示設計來改變收斂壓力來探究機制，顯示出兩種不同的狀態：由訓練數據先驗驅動的收斂（自然）對工件的擾動作出反應，而由明確指令驅動的收斂（誘導）則不會。這意味著上下文注入應該是有條件的，而不是普遍的：一次無上下文的試驗是一個廉價的診斷，可以預測知識工件是否會幫助或損害特定任務。
2605.04361.author: Saranyan Vigraham et.al.
